criteria.label: State where scaling sits relative to feature engineering, imputation, and encoding.
criteria.example: Write: engineer features → impute → encode categoricals → scale continuous numerics (if algorithm needs it) → model.

criteria.label: Name two algorithm families that need a scale branch and one that usually omits it.
criteria.example: Need scale branch: k-NN, Ridge. Usually omit: RandomForest on engineered numerics.

criteria.label: Sketch a ColumnTransformer with separate numeric and categorical branches.
criteria.example: Numeric: SimpleImputer → StandardScaler. Categorical: SimpleImputer → OneHotEncoder. Concatenate before estimator.

criteria.label: Explain why engineered ratios may still need scaling before k-NN.
criteria.example: `revenue_per_session` in hundreds vs `sessions_per_week` in single digits—distance dominated by dollars without StandardScaler.

criteria.label: Point to the canonical topic for StandardScaler formula details.
criteria.example: Link to data-preprocessing--scaling-and-normalization for z = (x − μ) / σ; FE topic owns pipeline wiring only.
